result:
**Goal: Add a Simulation Layer Compositing System** — stack 2-4 independent simulations as transparent layers with configurable blend modes (add, XOR, mask, multiply), each running at its own tick rate, composited into a single viewport in real-time.

This follows the project's recent strategic pattern of building "horizontal" meta-features that multiply the value of all 100+ existing modes. Portal connects two sims spatially at a seam; Mashup couples two sims on one grid globally. Compositing is the missing third axis: **depth**. Simulations run independently on separate layers and are visually merged via blend operations, producing emergent visual patterns without any simulation coupling. Think Photoshop layers, but live. A Reaction-Diffusion texture masked by a Game of Life creates organic breathing shapes; a Wave Equation added to Boids produces shimmering flocks; Fractal growth XOR'd with Lightning produces crystalline electrical trees.

Want me to build it?
